title: MCP Contract Dump
description: Complete capability dump of an MCP server conforming to specification 2025-06-18 or 2025-11-25

paginationDetectionResult.description: Pagination detection result for a capability. Tracks whether pagination was used and how many pages/items were retrieved.

paginationDetected.description: Whether nextCursor was seen when fetching this capability (always true when this object is present)

pagesRetrieved.description: Number of pages fetched to get all items for this capability

totalItems.description: Total number of items after exhaustive fetch

icon.description: An optionally-sized icon for UI display (MCP 2025-11-25+)

src.description: URI pointing to an icon resource (HTTP/HTTPS URL or data: URI)

sizes.description: Sizes at which the icon can be used (e.g., '48x48', '96x96', 'any')

theme.description: Theme this icon is designed for

version.description: Reference to the MCP dump schema version used to create this dump

toolName.description: Name of the tool used to create the dump

description.description: Optional human-readable description of how this dump was created or its purpose

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the dump was created

mcpServerConfig.description: Configuration used to connect to the MCP server

name.description: Human-readable name of the server (auto-generated if not provided)

transport.description: Transport type used to connect (always 'streamable-http' for HTTP, never 'http')

dumpExecution.description: Runtime information discovered during the connection and initialization with the MCP server

mcpProtocolUsed.description: MCP protocol version used by the dump tool when communicating with the server. This indicates which protocol semantics were used during the dump process and affects the structure and interpretation of the dump.

sessionIdSupported.description: Whether the server returned a session ID during initialization. Only applicable for streamable-http transport. If true, the server is stateful and requires session tracking. If false, the server is stateless and does not use session management.

sessionIdHeader.description: The exact header name (with casing) used by the server for session management. REQUIRED when sessionIdSupported is true. This captures the actual header name observed in the server's HTTP initialization response, preserving exact casing as returned by the server. Common values: 'Mcp-Session-Id' (2025-03-26, 2025-06-18 spec), 'MCP-Session-Id' (2025-11-25 spec), 'mcp-session-id' (some SDK implementations). While HTTP headers are case-insensitive per RFC 7230, this field preserves exact server behavior for accurate client implementation, documentation, and gateway configuration.

clientCapabilitiesSent.description: Client capabilities that were sent by the dump tool during initialization. This documents what the client declared it supports, which may influence server behavior in some implementations. Useful for understanding the full initialization handshake context.

corsSupport.description: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) support detection for browser-based MCP clients. Only applicable for streamable-http and sse transports. Captures CORS response headers and preflight test results to determine if the server can be used from browser-based tools like MCP Inspector. Session information is captured in dumpExecution.sessionIdSupported and dumpExecution.sessionIdHeader.

browserReady.description: Overall assessment: can this server be used from a browser? True if: 1) session header (if used) is exposed via Access-Control-Expose-Headers, 2) preflight allows POST method, 3) preflight allows session header (if used). False if any requirement is missing. Null if CORS detection was skipped or failed. Uses dumpExecution.sessionIdSupported and dumpExecution.sessionIdHeader for session checks.

responseHeaders.description: CORS headers from the initialize response

accessControlAllowOrigin.description: Value of Access-Control-Allow-Origin header (e.g., '*', 'https://example.com', or null if not present)

accessControlExposeHeaders.description: List of headers exposed to the browser via Access-Control-Expose-Headers

preflight.description: Results from OPTIONS preflight request (if performed)

tested.description: Whether a preflight request was sent

status.description: HTTP status code from OPTIONS request (typically 200 or 204 for success)

accessControlAllowOrigin.description: Value of Access-Control-Allow-Origin header from preflight

accessControlAllowMethods.description: List of methods allowed by preflight

accessControlAllowHeaders.description: List of headers allowed by preflight

pingSupported.description: Whether the server responded to a ping request. Ping is an optional MCP utility (spec 2025-11-25) used for connection health monitoring and session keepalive. True if server responded to ping, false if ping timed out or failed, undefined if ping was not tested. This information is useful for proxy/mock implementations and for understanding if coding agents can use ping for keepalive.

pingLatencyMs.description: Round-trip latency in milliseconds for the ping request. Represents baseline connection performance without business logic overhead. Only present when pingSupported is true. Useful for debugging slow connections and setting appropriate timeout values.

paginationSupport.description: Tracks which capabilities use pagination (cursor-based). Only includes capabilities where nextCursor was detected during fetching. Omitted entirely if no pagination was detected. This is zero-overhead detection - no extra API calls needed.

resourceTemplates.description: Pagination details for resource templates capability

serverInfo.description: Information about the MCP server from initialize response

title.description: Human-readable display name for UI contexts (MCP BaseMetadata, since 2025-06-18)

description.description: Human-readable description of what this server does (MCP Implementation, since 2025-06-18)

websiteUrl.description: URL of the server's website (MCP Implementation, since 2025-11-25)

icons.description: Optional set of sized icons for UI display (MCP Implementation, since 2025-11-25)

protocolVersion.description: MCP protocol version supported by server

completions.description: Present if the server supports argument autocompletion (MCP 2025-03-26+)

tasks.description: Present if the server supports task-augmented requests (MCP 2025-11-25+)

instructions.description: Optional instructions for using the server

tools.description: List of tools exposed by the server

title.description: Human-readable display name for UI contexts (MCP BaseMetadata, since 2025-06-18)

description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool does

tags.description: Tags for categorization (first tag used as primary category in documentation)

deprecated.description: Whether this tool is deprecated and should not be used in new code

inputSchema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters

outputSchema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's output (MCP 2025-06-18+) - structured response format

annotations.description: MCP tool annotations — hints about tool behavior (since 2025-03-26)

readOnlyHint.description: If true, the tool does not modify its environment. Default: false

destructiveHint.description: If true, the tool may perform destructive updates. Default: true

idempotentHint.description: If true, repeated calls with same arguments have no additional effect. Default: false

openWorldHint.description: If true, the tool may interact with external entities. Default: true

taskSupport.description: Whether this tool supports task-augmented execution. Default: forbidden

responseExamples.description: Named examples of tool responses (enrichment field) - helps developers understand typical outputs

name.description: Short name for this example (e.g., 'Success', 'Empty result', 'Error')

description.description: What this example demonstrates or when it occurs

resources.description: List of resources exposed by the server

title.description: Human-readable display name for UI contexts (MCP BaseMetadata, since 2025-06-18)

description.description: Description of what the resource contains

tags.description: Tags for categorization (first tag used as primary category in documentation)

deprecated.description: Whether this resource is deprecated and should not be used in new code

mimeType.description: MIME type of the resource content

size.description: Size of the raw resource content in bytes

contentExamples.description: Named examples of resource content (enrichment field) - helps developers understand typical content

name.description: Short name for this example (e.g., 'Typical session', 'Empty state')

resourceTemplates.description: List of resource templates (URI templates) exposed by the server

title.description: Human-readable display name for UI contexts (MCP BaseMetadata, since 2025-06-18)

description.description: Description of what resources this template provides

tags.description: Tags for categorization (first tag used as primary category in documentation)

deprecated.description: Whether this resource template is deprecated and should not be used in new code

mimeType.description: MIME type of resources created from this template

prompts.description: List of prompts exposed by the server

title.description: Human-readable display name for UI contexts (MCP BaseMetadata, since 2025-06-18)

description.description: Description of what the prompt does

tags.description: Tags for categorization (first tag used as primary category in documentation)

deprecated.description: Whether this prompt is deprecated and should not be used in new code

arguments.description: Arguments that can be passed to the prompt

title.description: Human-readable display name for UI contexts (MCP BaseMetadata, since 2025-06-18)

description.description: Description of what the argument does

roots.description: List of roots if client supports roots capability
